dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 2 for a priority 2 call at --Address Redacted--, Apt # 153: reported pregnancy.
Hi, I'm Kayla; I'm with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
I'm having severe abdominal and back pain and started bleeding.
Can you tell me your name, and where we are?
I'm Maria. We are in Apt #153, [CITY].
What day is it today?
March 25th, 2020.
What happened today that made you call 911?
Woke up with vaginal bleeding and sharp lower abdominal pain.
I see you have a patent airway. I just want to do some quick checks.
Ma'am, I'll listen to your lungs now. I need you to breathe deeply.
Respirations are 18. No signs of cyanosis. Air entry appears equal bilaterally.
Pulse is strong and regular. Arms and legs look warm and dry.
How long have you had these symptoms, and is the pain constant or intermittent?
It started about 10 minutes ago, sharp, and 10 out of 10.
Any other symptoms like dizziness, chest pain, or nausea?
No chest pain or nausea.
Has this happened to you before, or this is first time during pregnancy?
I had a miscarriage a few years back but not this bad.
Any other medical conditions or medications you take regularly?
I have anxiety, depression, and PTSD. I take ibuprofen occasionally.
Where exactly is the pain located, and does it radiate?
Lower abdomen mainly, and shoots up to my back.
Any triggers or activities that make it worse or better?
Standing doesn't help at all. I've been trying to sit or lie but had trouble.
How long has the pain existed, and did it start suddenly or gradually?
It started suddenly as I woke, about 10 minutes ago.
Just confirming, are you alert and aware of where you are?
Yes, I know where I am and what's happening.
Skin is warm, dry and normal in color. Capillary refill less than two seconds.
Pupils are reactive and equal, no visible swelling or masses on the neck.
Alertness seems unaffected, and memory appears intact.
Are you experiencing any pain or tenderness on palpation?
Not really, just pain lower.
Let me check the rest of your history-this is your second pregnancy, right?
Yes. I was G2, P0, A1-miscarriage two years ago.
First, I'll get your pulse now. It's elevated at 113 beats per minute.
Respirations are 18. No labored effort observed.
Blood pressure is currently 129 over 78.
Oxygen saturation is 97%.
Alright, we're doing a four-lead EKG now to get a baseline heart rhythm.
Looks like it's appearing as sinus rhythm despite movement, no arrhythmia noted initially.
Unfortunately, our glucometer isn't reading, but we note glucose 0 under logs for documentation.
Based on what we see, we're following protocol 3-2: medical - abdominal pain.
We'll keep the EKG on for now to track any changes in the rhythm.
Let me check your pulse again-it has decreased slightly to 101 now.
Second reading shows 134 over 57, still within normal range.
Your oxygen saturation is now slightly higher, at 99%.
Since you don't want to go to your regular prenatal hospital, are you okay with transportation to [COMPANY_NAME]?
We'll get you onto the stretcher now. Lay back, I'll secure you in safely.
Just get me lying so I can rest.
Got it. We'll put you onto the stretcher with a 5-point harness.
You're secure now. We're starting toward the hospital.
I'm calling ahead now to inform [COMPANY_NAME] they can expect us within 15 minutes.
You remain fully alert and oriented throughout transport-no change.
Per visual exam, Glascow score is 15-eye 4, verbal 5, motor 6.
EKG rhythm changes on screen, but it's likely a movement issue. Leads keep disconnecting.
Glucometer still isn't working, but no signs of low blood sugar or deterioration.
The patient is alert and stable. We are transporting at a non-emergent pace.
I'm here with you. You are getting care on the way to the hospital for your symptoms.
I'll stay by your side until we hand you off.
Delivered to the 6th floor directly for exam. No issues with transport or decontamination.
They are expecting us with a full report. All vitals, procedures, and history transferred.
No heavy bleeding at this point and no decline during transport.
She is 24 weeks, with history of miscarriage, depression, and anxiety-no new mental status issues.
Full report, EKG, vitals, medications, and PTSD and depression history passed to RN.
Safe transport completed. Final status: Alert, oriented, pain remains 10/10.
